latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6635Y
Nuformix PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Nuformix plc
("Nuformix" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
Following the issue of new ordinary shares of 0.05p each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the placing, as announced on 11 March 2026, the Company is today providing a confirmation regarding its voting rights, in line with section DTR 5.6.1 of the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules sourcebook.
As of the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 2,609,749,903 Ordinary Shares, each with one voting right. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total voting rights figure is therefore 2,609,749,903 and this can be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change of their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority.
Enquiries:
Nuformix plc
Dr Dan Gooding, Executive Director
Via IFC Advisory

+44 (0) 20 3003 8632
IFC Advisory Limited
Tim Metcalfe
Zach Cohen

nuformix@investor-focus.co.uk
About Nuformix
Nuformix is a pharmaceutical development company targeting unmet medical needs in fibrosis and oncology via drug repurposing. The Company aims to use its expertise in discovering, developing and patenting novel drug forms, with improved physical properties, to develop new products in new indications that are, importantly, differentiated from the original (by way of dosage, delivery route or presentation), thus creating new and attractive commercial opportunities. Nuformix has a pipeline of preclinical assets with potential for significant value and early licensing opportunities.
For more information, please visit
www.nuformix.com
.

latest.title: £1.0 million Placing
latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1290W
Nuformix PLC
11 March 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT AND THE INFORMATION IN IT, IS RESTRICTED AND IS NOT FOR PUBLICATION, RELEASE OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES, AUSTRALIA, CANADA, THE REPUBLIC OF SOUTH AFRICA, JAPAN, NEW ZEALAND, SINGAPORE OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH PUBLICATION, RELEASE OR DISTRIBUTION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF EU REGULATION 596/2014 (WHICH FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC UK LAW PURSUANT TO THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("EUWA")) ("UK MAR"). IN ADDITION, MARKET SOUNDINGS (AS DEFINED IN UK MAR) WERE TAKEN IN RESPECT OF CERTAIN OF THE MATTERS CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT, WITH THE RESULT THAT CERTAIN PERSONS BECAME AWARE OF INSIDE INFORMATION (AS DEFINED UNDER UK MAR). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THOSE PERSONS WHO RECEIVED INSIDE INFORMATION IN A MARKET SOUNDING ARE NO LONGER IN POSSESSION OF SUCH INSIDE INFORMATION, WHICH IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
11 March 2026
Nuformix plc
("Nuformix" or the "Company" or the "Group")
£1.0 million Placing
Nuformix plc (LSE:NFX), a pharmaceutical development company targeting unmet medical needs in fibrosis and oncology via drug repurp
osing, announces a placing (the "Placing")
to raise gross proceeds of £1.0 million through the issue of 500,000,000 new ordinary shares ("New Ordinary Shares") in the capital of the Company at a price of 0.20 pence per share (the "Issue Price").
The net proceeds of the Placing will be used by the Company to drive forward its NXP002 programme, an inhaled treatment for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis ("IPF") and progressive pulmonary fibrosis ("PPF") and for general corporate purposes. In particular, the net proceeds will be used to fund a focused programme of additional pre-clinical studies to reinforce areas identified during ongoing out-licensing discussions and due diligence with both large pharmaceutical and speciality pharma partners, and to further advance the NXP002 programme. Whilst completion of the focused studies will represent important value inflection points for the NXP002 programme it is not expected that all the studies will need to conclude for an out-licensing or collaborative development transaction to complete.
CMC Markets UK Plc, trading as CMC CapX ("CMC Markets"), acted as the Company's sole placing agent in respect of the Placing.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
The Placing has been conducted utilising the Directors existing authorities to allot shares. The New Ordinary Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing ordinary shares. The Placing is conditional, inter alia, on there being no breach of the obligations under the Placing Agreement entered into between CMC Markets and the Company prior to Admission, and admission of the New Ordinary Shares to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange ("Admission") becoming effective.
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange and the Equity Shares (Transition) category of the FCA Official List. It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the New Ordinary Shares on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange will commence on or around 17 March 2026.
Following Admission of the New Ordinary Shares, the total number of ordinary shares in issue will be 2,609,749,903, each with one voting right. The Company does not hold any rights in treasury. The total voting rights figure is therefore 2,609,749,903 and can be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change of their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority.

latest.title: Confirmation of US ODD for NXP002 in IPF
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9473V
Nuformix PLC
10 March 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF REGULATION 2014/596/EU WHICH IS PART OF DOMESTIC UK LAW AS AMENDED BY THE MARKET ABUSE (AMENDMENT) (EU EXIT) REGULATIONS (
SI 2019/310
) PURSUANT TO THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("UK MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION (AS DEFINED IN UK MAR) IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
10 March 2026
Nuformix plc
("Nuformix" or the "Company")
Confirmation of US Orphan Drug Designation for NXP002 Programme in IPF
Nuformix plc (LSE:NFX), a pharmaceutical development company targeting unmet medical needs in fibrosis and oncology via drug repurposing, is pleased to announce that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") has granted Orphan Drug Designation ("ODD")
in Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis ("IPF") for tranilast lystate, the active drug substance enabled for inhaled delivery in Nuformix's NXP002 lead programme
.
The FDA grants ODD for investigational treatments for rare diseases, such as IPF, defined as affecting fewer than 200,000 people in the United States. ODD qualifies the developer for certain incentives with the goal of accelerating drug development for patients, including tax credits for clinical trials or qualified clinical testing costs, a waiver of the Prescription Drug User Fee Act application fee when a marketing application is submitted, and the potential to receive seven years of marketing exclusivity upon product approval.
Dr Dan Gooding, Executive Director, Nuformix, said:
"
We are delighted to receive confirmation that Orphan Drug Designation has been granted by the FDA for our NXP002 programme in IPF,
a high-mortality rare disease, in urgent need of new treatments.
The granting of ODD in the United States, alongside the European EMA ODD granted in April 2025, will be valuable for the potential future licensing partners we are in discussions with and I look forward to providing further updates in due course."
Enquiries:
Nuformix plc
Dr Dan Gooding, Executive Director
Via IFC Advisory
CMC Markets
Douglas Crippen
+44 (0) 20 3003 8632
IFC Advisory Limited
Tim Metcalfe
Zach Cohen
+44 (0) 20 3934 6632
nuformix@investor-focus.co.uk
About Nuformix
Nuformix is a pharmaceutical development company targeting unmet medical needs in fibrosis and oncology via drug repurposing. The Company aims to use its expertise in discovering, developing and patenting novel drug forms, with improved physical properties, to develop new products in new indications that are, importantly, differentiated from the original (by way of dosage, delivery route or presentation), thus creating new and attractive commercial opportunities. Nuformix has a pipeline of preclinical assets with potential for significant value and early licensing opportunities.
About IPF
IPF is a chronic lung disease characterised by progressive tissue scarring that prevents proper lung function. It is a progressive, fatal, age-associated lung disease affecting approximately three out of every one hundred thousand people in Europe. IPF typically presents in adults 65 or older and is usually fatal within two to five years after diagnosis.

company_30s.what_they_do: Nuformix PLC develops inhaled treatments that target the root cause of cystic fibrosis and other chronic lung diseases. Their lead product, NXP002, is a novel drug delivery platform designed to improve drug deposition and reduce systemic side‑effects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The respiratory disease market is worth billions and still has a large unmet need for effective, patient‑friendly therapies. Nuformix’s technology could fill a niche in severe cystic fibrosis where current inhalers are ineffective, giving the company a competitive edge if regulatory hurdles are cleared.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑stage clinical pipeline, raising capital to fund trials and regulatory submissions. It is operating at a cash‑burn rate typical of biotech startups, and its shares are trading near the bottom of their historical range.

rns_narrative: Nuformix has been actively raising funds and securing regulatory milestones. Over the past months it completed a £1 million placing, announced a US FDA orphan drug designation for NXP002, and confirmed a European orphan drug status. These moves signal that the company is moving from fundraising into formal approval pathways.
The company has also been updating governance and audit teams, indicating a focus on compliance as it prepares for larger clinical commitments.
Collectively, the narrative shows a firm that is building the necessary regulatory and financial foundation to push its product toward market.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is low, but pockets of hope appear around potential regulatory approval.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are bullish, citing an analyst’s 12‑month target of £10.00 for the stock. They see the company’s FDA and orphan drug milestones as the tipping point.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of substantive clinical data and the risk that the drug could fail in later trials. They also note that the company’s high burn rate could force a costly recapitalization.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has shifted from neutral to a cautious optimism, but overall engagement remains minimal. No major spikes in sentiment are seen.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat, indicating that the public is not currently focused on Nuformix. This silence can amplify surprise if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: Positive interim results from the Phase 2 trial of NXP002 – a clear win‑rate or significant reduction in lung function decline would be a trigger.
A formal FDA advisory panel endorsement or a decision letter confirming the orphan drug designation.
A partnership or licensing agreement with a larger pharma that could provide capital and distribution support.
A sharp uptick in trading volume that signals institutional buying ahead of a news release.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in volume accompanied by a price move above the 20‑day moving average. Also monitor the company’s press releases for any mention of trial milestones or regulatory updates. A sudden drop in volume after a positive announcement could signal a lack of conviction.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery cycle – the crash is deep and the market has largely moved on. Confirmation will likely come when a regulatory milestone is announced or when trial data is released. Until then, the market remains in a holding pattern.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Speculative Long – the deep crash offers a potential for a large rebound, but the lack of a confirmed signal colour means the bet is still high risk.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If NXP002 clears the next regulatory hurdle or shows a breakthrough in clinical data, the price could climb from its current low to the analyst‑projected £10.00, yielding a 5,000 % upside. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the company is already at a low valuation relative to its pipeline potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the drug fails in later trials or that regulatory authorities refuse to grant further approval. In that scenario the stock could fall to zero or near‑zero, wiping out the investment. The company’s high cash burn also means it could run out of runway before a breakthrough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: the entire invested capital if the drug fails. Potential gain: up to 5,000 % if the stock hits the £10.00 target. The asymmetry is heavily in favour of upside, but the downside is absolute.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average after a positive regulatory or trial announcement. Allocate a small portion of the portfolio (5–10 %) because of the high risk. Place a stop just below the recent low to protect against a sudden reversal.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume spike of at least 150 % over the 3‑day average paired with a price move above the 20‑day average. Also watch for an official FDA letter or a partnership announcement.
playbook.if_passing: No clear terminal risk is present, so the default position is to lean bullish on the deep‑crash setup until a catalyst materialises.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Nuformix sits at a deep crash, has built regulatory momentum, and could see a massive upside if its inhaled therapy clears the next approval hurdle – but it remains a high‑risk, high‑reward play.
